data.title: Consolidation Trends in UK Real Estate Market
data.description: The UK's publicly traded real estate sector is experiencing significant consolidation as investors aim for larger and more cost-effective companies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the trend where investors are increasingly favoring bigger firms that offer economies of scale and reduced operational costs. This shift is driven by the desire for more stable investments amid fluctuating market conditions. The consolidation wave reflects broader economic strategies as stakeholders seek to optimize their portfolios in the face of ongoing financial uncertainties. The real estate market's transformation is indicative of a strategic pivot towards efficiency and resilience in investment choices.

data.title: Iran Appoints New Secretary for Supreme National Security Council
data.description: Iran has appointed Mohammad Baqer Zolqadr as the new secretary of its Supreme National Security Council, according to Jin10. This appointment marks a significant change in the leadership of Iran's top security body, which plays a crucial role in shaping the country's defense and foreign policies. The Supreme National Security Council is responsible for coordinating Iran's national security policies and strategies, and Zolqadr's appointment is expected to influence the direction of these policies. The council's decisions are pivotal in addressing both domestic and international security challenges faced by Iran.

data.title: NagaCorp Reports Significant Profit Increase and Resumes Dividend
data.description:
NagaCorp Holdings has announced a substantial increase in its annual profit attributable to shareholders, reaching $310 million by the end of last year, marking a 183% rise compared to the previous year. According to Ming Pao, this marks the first time since the pandemic that the company has resumed its final dividend, set at $0.109 or 8.45 cents.

Today, the company's stock price rose by 6.1%, making it the best-performing stock in the gaming sector. It returned to the HKD 4 mark, closing at HKD 4.17, with a trading volume of HKD 29.5 million.

data.title: Stellantis Refocuses on Product Enhancement Following Cost Reductions
data.description: Stellantis has announced a strategic shift towards enhancing its product offerings after undergoing a challenging phase of cost-cutting measures. Bloomberg posted on X that the automotive giant is prioritizing improvements in its vehicle lineup to better meet consumer demands and strengthen its market position. This move comes as the company aims to balance financial efficiency with innovation and quality in its products. Stellantis, formed from the merger of Fiat Chrysler and PSA Group, is focusing on leveraging its diverse brand portfolio to drive growth and competitiveness in the global automotive industry. The decision to refocus on product development highlights the company's commitment to delivering value to its customers while navigating the complexities of the current economic landscape.

data.title: Oil Prices Expected to Rise Amid Tensions in Hormuz Strait
data.description:
Oil prices are projected to remain between $85 and $90 per barrel, with a potential increase to $110, according to ChainCatcher. This forecast comes despite possible easing tensions following a statement by U.S. President Donald Trump on Monday. The full reopening of the Hormuz Strait is necessary for prices to stabilize.

If the strait remains closed, Brent crude prices could reach $150 per barrel by the end of April, with refined oil prices expected to be 200% higher than pre-conflict levels.

Currently, oil transport through the strait is at 1.5 million barrels per day, significantly lower than the normal 15 million barrels per day, indicating a daily supply shortfall of 13.5 million barrels.

data.title: U.S. Supports India's Defense Production Goals, Says Deputy Defense Secretary
data.description: U.S. Deputy Defense Secretary Colby stated that the United States supports India's objective to enhance its domestic defense production capabilities. According to Jin10, this support aligns with broader strategic interests in strengthening defense ties between the two nations. The collaboration aims to bolster India's defense industry, contributing to regional stability and security. This move is part of a larger effort to deepen defense cooperation and technological exchange between the U.S. and India.

data.title: Global Economy Faces Initial Signs of Synchronized Shock
data.description:
The global economy is showing early indications of a synchronized shock, according to recent business surveys. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that these surveys reveal a downturn in manufacturing and services sectors across major economies. This development raises concerns about the potential for a broader economic slowdown.

The surveys indicate that both developed and emerging markets are experiencing a decline in business activity. In particular, the manufacturing sector is facing significant challenges, with output and new orders decreasing. The services sector is also under pressure, with growth slowing in several key regions.

Economists are closely monitoring these trends, as they could signal a more widespread economic downturn. The synchronized nature of the slowdown is particularly concerning, as it suggests that global economic challenges are becoming more interconnected.

Analysts are urging policymakers to take proactive measures to address these issues and prevent a deeper economic crisis. The situation underscores the importance of coordinated international efforts to stabilize the global economy and support growth.

data.title: Binance to Launch Beta Version of AI Pro Trading Assistant
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the company is set to introduce the Beta version of Binance Ai Pro, an advanced artificial intelligence trading assistant, on 2026-03-25 07:00 (UTC). This innovative tool is built on the OpenClaw open-source ecosystem and can be accessed via the Binance Ai feature on the Android app or through the Binance web homepage. The AI Pro aims to streamline trading operations with features like one-click activation and automatic cloud-based service setup, allowing users to configure models and link trading accounts seamlessly.

The AI models integrated into Binance Ai Pro include advanced engines such as ChatGPT, Claude, Qwen, MiniMax, and Kimi, along with third-party AI skills and Binance Skills. Upon activation, a virtual sub-account, known as the Binance Ai Pro Account, is created and linked with an AI API key. This key is designed with no withdrawal or transfer permissions to minimize operational risks and ensure fund segregation between the main Binance account and the AI Pro Account. Users can manually transfer funds to the AI Account, enabling Binance Ai Pro to execute strategies, trades, and asset monitoring functions.

The Beta version will initially be available to a limited number of users, with plans for a gradual rollout to more users. Activation is currently supported on Android, with iOS support expected soon. The initial activation charge during the Beta phase is $9.99 per month, discounted from the regular $29.99/month fee, payable via Binance Pay. A 7-day free trial is offered for first-time activators, with 5 million usage credits provided monthly. These credits allow access to advanced AI models, and once exhausted, the system defaults to basic models to ensure continued operations. Security measures are in place to limit AI API key permissions, enhancing account management and mitigating risks. Users are advised to use AI products responsibly, as Binance does not control specific AI trading actions or provide strategies or advice.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Nornickel Seeks New Applications for Palladium Amid Electric Vehicle Shift
data.description:
Nornickel is actively exploring new applications for palladium as the rise of electric vehicles diminishes its traditional demand. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's efforts to adapt to changing market dynamics. Palladium, primarily used in catalytic converters for gasoline-powered vehicles, faces reduced demand as the automotive industry shifts towards electric vehicles, which do not require such converters.

In response, Nornickel is investigating alternative uses for palladium in various industries, including electronics and hydrogen energy. The company aims to diversify its market presence and mitigate the impact of declining automotive demand. Nornickel's strategic move reflects broader industry trends as companies seek to innovate and find new applications for materials affected by technological advancements.

The transition to electric vehicles poses challenges for palladium producers, prompting them to explore new avenues for growth. Nornickel's initiative underscores the importance of adapting to evolving market conditions and finding sustainable solutions for the future.

data.title: JustLend DAO Reports $6.43 Billion Total Value Locked in TRON Ecosystem
data.description: JustLend DAO has announced that its total value locked has reached $6.43 billion, according to its latest official weekly report. The decentralized lending platform, which operates within the TRON ecosystem, also reported total deposits amounting to $3.5 billion. According to NS3.AI, this development highlights the growing adoption and utilization of decentralized finance solutions within the TRON network.

data.title: Stocks Fluctuate Amid Iran Developments
data.description: Global stock markets are experiencing volatility as they react to the latest developments concerning Iran. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the uncertainty that has led to fluctuations in stock prices. Investors are closely monitoring the situation, which has introduced a level of unpredictability in the markets. The ongoing geopolitical tensions are influencing trading decisions, causing stocks to oscillate between gains and losses. Market analysts suggest that this trend may continue as long as the situation remains unresolved.

data.title: OKX launches the Agent Trading Course AI 101 to help users get started with AI trading from scratch.
data.description: According to official news, OKX has officially launched its AI Agent Trading Introductory Course, "OKX AI 101." This course is designed for users interested in AI trading, employing a free self-study model. It covers 12 lessons and includes a complete process with quizzes and a certificate of completion. Combining OKX's Agent Trade Kit and Onchain OS, both AI-powered tools, it helps users progress from beginner to advanced levels of practical application. OKX AI 101 focuses on practical AI Agent trading, emphasizing "learn and use immediately," aiming to lower the barrier to entry for AI trading and promote users' mastery of the practical application of AI tools in crypto trading.

data.title: ECB's Kazaks: Interest Rate Hike Needed if Inflation Spreads Beyond Energy
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) may need to raise interest rates if inflation extends beyond the energy sector, according to ECB Governing Council member Martins Kazaks. According to Jin10, Kazaks emphasized the importance of monitoring inflation trends closely, as the current economic environment remains uncertain. He noted that while energy prices have been a significant driver of inflation, any spillover into other sectors could necessitate a policy response. Kazaks' comments come amid ongoing discussions within the ECB about the appropriate monetary policy stance to address rising inflationary pressures. The ECB has been cautious in its approach, balancing the need to support economic recovery with the risk of persistent inflation. Kazaks highlighted the need for flexibility in policy decisions, suggesting that the ECB must be prepared to adjust its strategy as economic conditions evolve. The potential for inflation to spread beyond energy underscores the complexity of the current economic landscape and the challenges faced by central banks in maintaining price stability.

data.title: Israel fears Trump will compromise to declare victory.
data.description: Starting early this morning (March 24) local time, Iran launched seven rounds of missiles at Israel, covering most of the country. One missile hit downtown Tel Aviv, damaging three buildings and injuring several people. Regarding US President Trump's remarks yesterday that he was "negotiating with Iran," Israeli media analysts say that Trump's statement was "somewhat unexpected" for Israel, and that Israel fears Trump might compromise in negotiations "to declare victory." (CCTV International News)

data.title: Iran makes five ceasefire demands to the United States
data.description: Iran has presented the United States with five ceasefire demands, including: closing all U.S. military bases in the Persian Gulf; providing full compensation for damaged Iranian infrastructure; recognizing Iran's control over the Strait of Hormuz; lifting all secondary sanctions; and a guarantee of non-interference in Iran's internal affairs. Furthermore, although the U.S. claims negotiations are underway, Tehran denies any ongoing negotiations. (Solid Intel)

data.title: U.S. Military B-52H Bomber Issues Emergency Signal Over UK
data.description: A U.S. military B-52H 'Stratofortress' bomber issued a '7700' emergency signal east of Bristol, UK, according to a report by 24-hour flight radar on the 24th. According to Jin10, the emergency signal indicates a general emergency situation, prompting heightened alert and response measures. The B-52H is a long-range, subsonic, jet-powered strategic bomber that has been a key component of the U.S. Air Force's strategic bombing capabilities. The cause of the emergency signal has not been disclosed, and further details on the incident are awaited.

data.title: Indian Refiners Reduce Palm and Soybean Oil Purchases Amid Expected Price Stabilization
data.description: Indian refiners have decreased their purchases of palm and soybean oil, anticipating a stabilization in oil prices driven by the ongoing conflict, according to Jin10. Traders report that the decision comes as the market expects the surge in oil prices, influenced by geopolitical tensions, to ease. This adjustment in procurement strategy reflects a broader sentiment among industry players who are closely monitoring the situation and its potential impact on commodity prices. The move by Indian refiners is seen as a precautionary measure to mitigate risks associated with volatile market conditions.

data.title: Cardano's Recent Performance: Analyst Highlights Historical Gains
data.description: Cardano (ADA) has shown significant growth following its dips below $0.25 in previous years. According to NS3.AI, crypto analyst Ali Martinez noted that ADA experienced gains of 85.11% and 200.54% after falling below this price point in 2022 and 2023, respectively. Currently, ADA is trading at $0.2648, marking an increase of 6.63%.

data.title: UK Government Plans £7.4 Billion Annual Investment in Small Businesses by 2028
data.description: The UK government has announced plans to invest £7.4 billion annually in small businesses by 2028. According to Jin10, this initiative aims to bolster the growth and sustainability of small enterprises across the country. The investment is part of a broader strategy to enhance economic resilience and support innovation within the small business sector. The government believes that by providing substantial financial backing, small businesses will be better equipped to navigate economic challenges and contribute to the overall economic development of the UK. This move is expected to create new opportunities for entrepreneurs and stimulate job creation, ultimately strengthening the country's economic landscape.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent Expresses Concerns Over Rising National Debt
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent has consistently voiced his concerns about the increasing national debt, which motivated him to transition from a desk job to public service. Bloomberg posted on X that recent developments have intensified these concerns, amplifying the urgency of addressing the nation's fiscal challenges. Bessent's apprehensions highlight the ongoing debate over the sustainability of the U.S. debt and the potential economic implications if left unaddressed. The situation underscores the need for strategic fiscal policies to manage and mitigate the growing financial burden.
